NetApp investor relations / press releases (no material releases after June 6)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down already discounted the post-earnings rerating (+53% YTD, Goldman/Argus $200 targets = full consensus recognition); today's -4.3% is flow-driven digestion with no new fundamental news, not enough giveback to restore hidden-alpha asymmetry, so hold with mild drift down.
verdicts.bull: AI/agentic data-gravity demand is real: 10th straight quarter of growth, record 32% operating margins, double-digit cloud growth, and Cisco/Splunk deals broaden the enterprise AI storage footprint.
verdicts.neutral: Strong operator, but the May 29 rerating priced in the AI storage narrative; at ~$32B cap with sell-side targets at $200, the name is now a recognized AI beneficiary, not a hidden bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Enterprise storage stays competitive and cyclical (Pure, VAST, Dell, hyperscaler-native storage); if AI infra capex digests, a +53% YTD name that just rerated on one print has the most multiple to give back.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q1 FY27 guidance/print confirming AI storage and Keystone/cloud momentum can compound beyond the one-quarter beat.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating crowding: any AI capex digestion or storage pricing pressure unwinds the fresh multiple faster than fundamentals deteriorate.
